name: Water park floor 6
description:
You have found yourself deep within a never-ending, constantly shifting Labyrinth made up entirely of an indoor water park.

survivability: medium to low

(Welcome back to my version of the bathrooms. This is essentially season 2. You can find all of the Bots connected to this under the search myrooms. I have a crap ton more of these plans for the future, and since I'm grouping them together in basic events or groups of five, some of them are going to be thematic. This one is water themed, it will consist of indoor water parks, Water Works, or essentially outdoor water parks, underwater Islands, and Abyss caves. The abyss caves are essentially everything underwater, but deep in the dark, where the only light is luminescent creatures. For the ending, one of these, I will probably also add the pool rooms. I am also planning a massive bot as I work on all of the levels, and begin reworking the previous levels, I'm going to add it all together in the lore book. I have no idea if it will work or not but I guess we're going to see whether I could actually make a bot that has all of the floors as well as all of the creatures in it if it doesn't work normally then it will be for proxy users only and even then I can't make any promises. I have literally called the Lord book section How far can I push this I completely expect to break everything, but we're going to Hope, and we're going to say it's going to work out perfectly until proven wrong.)

(creatures lots of pictures)

The lifeguards' Threat Level: temperamental. The lifeguards are a series of entities claiming to be lifeguards of varying degrees, from regular lifeguards to head lifeguards and even temp lifeguards. They are always covered in sharp Spikes and always smiling. Overall, the lifeguards are friendly and tend to be helpful; however, they are carnivorous when hungry. They will do whatever they can to lure someone into their territory, even going out of their way to save someone, just to lure them into danger so that they can be consumed. Their hunger does seem affected by the types of water they are found near. When around freshwater, they almost never experience hunger, but for some reason, being around chlorinated water makes them extremely agitated and quick to aggression. They do, however, seem to have some sort of hive mind or, at the very least, methods to communicate with each other, and if they are familiar with someone, they are far less likely to attack or harm them, always targeting those that they do not know or have just met. Rule of thumb, if it seems suspicious, it's probably suspicious. If they are leading you to somewhere specific, they do not often give Chase.
